entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The crop species Arachis hypogaea L. used to evaluate varietal resistance to aflatoxin.

entities.name: AFLATOXIN B1 PRODUCTION
entities.description: The capacity of peanut genotypes to support the synthesis of aflatoxin B1 by Aspergillus flavus.

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS FLAVUS
entities.description: The aflatoxigenic fungal strain used to inoculate peanut seeds.

entities.name: INOCULATION
entities.description: The method of introducing Aspergillus flavus into scarified peanut seeds.

entities.name: INCUBATION
entities.description: The method of maintaining inoculated seeds at a specific temperature for a set period to allow fungal growth.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The environmental condition of 25 C used during the incubation process.

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The environmental condition of the rainy season affecting seed production and aflatoxin levels.

entities.name: POSTRAINY SEASON
entities.description: The environmental condition of the irrigated postrainy season affecting seed production and aflatoxin levels.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The geographic region associated with the commonly grown peanut cultivar TMV 2.

entities.name: VARIETAL RESISTANCE
entities.description: The concept of genetic differences among peanut genotypes in their ability to limit aflatoxin B1 production.
